books.title: My Ántonia
books.author: Willa Cather
books.summary: Orphaned boy Jim meets Ántonia, the daughter of a Czech family who has immigrated to Nebraska.

books.title: The Coral Island
books.author: R. M. Ballantyne
books.summary: Three shipwrecked youths encounter delights and dangers as they attempt to escape their uninhabited tropical island paradise.

books.title: The Autobiography of Mark Twain
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A collection of reminiscences and reflections from the author’s experiences.

books.title: Tombstone
books.author: Walter Noble Burns
books.summary: An embellished retelling of the town of Tombstone, Arizona, during its heyday in the late 19th century, featuring famous heroes and outlaws of the American Old West.

books.title: A Little Princess
books.author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
books.summary: A young girl discovers a life of unexpected challenges when she goes to live at a London boarding school.

books.title: Mr. Mulliner Stories
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A collection of short stories featuring the eccentric (and hilarious) Mr. Mulliner, who regales the patrons of the Anglers’ Rest with tales of his colorful and amusing relatives.

books.title: The Inspector General
books.author: Nikolai Gogol
books.summary: Corrupt officials in a small Russian town are thrown into disarray by the arrival of an Inspector General from the central government.

books.title: The Well at the World’s End
books.author: William Morris
books.summary: A king’s son embarks on an adventure to find a legendary well that grants long life.

books.title: Our Nig
books.author: Harriet E. Wilson
books.summary: An autobiographical account of a young biracial girl abandoned by her parents and forced into servitude in the northern United States.

books.title: The Three Impostors
books.author: Arthur Machen
books.summary: Two flâneurs in Victorian London get caught up with the members of a secret society and their search for a rare Roman coin.

books.title: Up from Slavery
books.author: Booker T. Washington
books.summary: The autobiography of Booker T. Washington, former slave and founder of the Tuskegee Institute.

books.title: Tragedy at Ravensthorpe
books.author: J. J. Connington
books.summary: Chief Constable Sir Clinton Driffield investigates the theft of valuables from a country estate after a masquerade ball.

books.title: Sticks and Stones
books.author: Lewis Mumford
books.summary: Lewis Mumford discusses the effects of architecture on American society throughout history.

books.title: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
books.author: Mahatma Gandhi
books.summary: Gandhi relates his life experiences from his birth in Gujarat in 1869 through the Indian National Congress of 1915.

books.title: Don Juan
books.author: Lord Byron
books.summary: A Spanish libertine becomes an unwitting seducer through a series of adventurous escapades.

books.title: Sunshine Sketches of a Little Town
books.author: Stephen Leacock
books.summary: A humorous account of life in the fictional small town of Mariposa.

books.title: The Coming Race
books.author: Edward Bulwer-Lytton
books.summary: While exploring a mine, a man falls through a chasm into an underground world populated by a mysterious race.

books.title: Just So Stories
books.author: Rudyard Kipling
books.summary: A collection of short humorous fables for children, most purporting to tell the origin of animal characteristics such as the camel’s hump.

books.title: Religion and the Rise of Capitalism
books.author: R. H. Tawney
books.summary: A tracing of the development of religious thought from the Middle Ages to the dawn of the Industrial Revolution.

books.title: South!
books.author: Ernest Shackleton
books.summary: A British expedition sets off to cross Antarctica, but when their ship is wrecked they have to face a long struggle for survival.
